purpose: Source-reviewed monster stage side effects, including death progression and world shake, owned by StageController rather than animation or loot code.

architecture.enemy_role: AnimatedEnemy emits data-only source_stage_effect_requested signals at death-start or despawn.
architecture.stage_role: StageController applies door, spawn-chain, unique reward, merit, activity and the source Ending presentation.
architecture.reason: These effects mutate stage progression or player persistence and must not be hidden in an animation callback.

effects.behavior: Only the boss stage variant reveals transfer doors; normal variants do not.
Grant directly to player 1 only when no player owns jgz and player 1 has inventory capacity; never create a world pickup.
At most once per source day in Zmxiyou1SourceProgress.
Lock player external controls, show the original 940x590 Ending, replay symbol 701 at 24 Hz, destroy it on source frame 1204, then release the scoped lock and allow stage completion.
Call the player activity-equipment initializer and emit the stage request once.

screen_shake_contract.acceptance: ViewControllor.shake(value) accepts a new request only while shakeVal is zero; overlapping requests are ignored.
screen_shake_contract.motion: On the first source tick gameSence.x += strength; on the next source tick gameSence.x -= strength and shakeVal returns to zero.
screen_shake_contract.godot_target: StageController offsets the configured Node2D world root, which includes gameplay actors and stage art while CanvasLayer HUD remains stable.
screen_shake_contract.cleanup: Any outstanding additive offset is restored if the stage is cleared or removed.

chain_completion_guard.behavior: Pending or active M22 -> M23 -> M24 chain IDs prevent stage completion between boss phases; M24 completion remains deferred while the source Ending is active.

ending_visual.resource_contract: Four one-frame sprite.png + sprite.json packs plus exact per-frame transform data; no duplicate full-frame bake.

validation.coverage: M04 exact 16-frame shake dispatch, two-tick world restoration, overlap rejection, cleanup restoration, M24 shared shake path, M24 reward idempotency, control lock, source mask, exact title/story registration, frame-1204 teardown and deferred completion.
